concerns.text: Tasks T020-T023 (metric/shift implementation) are marked [P] and placed before T024 (evaluation loop). However, T024/T025 consume the outputs of T020-T023. While the file paths suggest T020-T023 are utility functions, the task descriptions imply implementation of logic that T024 calls. If T024 is implemented before T020-T023 are complete, it will fail. More critically, T020-T023 are marked [P] (parallel-safe), but they all write to/modify `code/utils/metrics.py` and `code/utils/shift_detection.py`. Multiple developers cannot safely implement T020, T021, T022, and T023 in parallel on the same files without merge conflicts or race conditions. The [P] tag is incorrect for tasks modifying the same shared files.

Tasks T029-T032 (statistical analysis) are listed before T033-T035 (report generation). T033-T035 depend on the outputs of T029-T032 (p-values, coefficients, plots data). The current order respects this dependency. However, T029-T032 are not marked [P] but T033-T035 are not either. T033 and T034 (plot generation) depend on the data processed in T029-T032. T035 (report assembly) depends on T033 and T034. The ordering is correct, but the lack of explicit dependency markers or grouping might confuse parallel execution. The main issue is the [P] tag on T020-T023 which is a hard violation of parallel safety.

Tasks T010 and T011 (tests for US1) are listed before the implementation tasks T012-T017. While the 'Implementation Strategy' section mentions 'Verify tests fail before implementing', the task list order implies tests are done first. This is a valid TDD approach, but the [P] tag on T010 and T011 is questionable if they depend on the schema defined in T004 (Foundational) and the data acquisition logic in T012-T014. If T010/T011 are written before T012-T014, they will fail to run or mock incorrectly. The [P] tag is acceptable if they are independent unit tests, but the dependency on T004 (schema) is implicit. Ensure T004 is completed before T010/T011.

Tasks T020, T021, T022, and T023 are all marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T020 and T021 both target `code/utils/metrics.py`, and T022 and T023 both target `code/utils/shift_detection.py`. Implementing multiple functions in the same file simultaneously by different developers is not parallel-safe due to file locking and merge conflict risks. The [P] tag is semantically incorrect for these tasks. They should be sequential or split into separate files to be truly parallel-safe.
